scenes.text: In this scene, we explore the basics of creating a JavaScript function. Functions are essential in programming as they allow code reusability and organization by encapsulating blocks of code designed to perform a particular task. The example provided demonstrates how to declare a function, invoke it, and output the result using the console.

This scene delves into the conceptual understanding of JavaScript function execution flow using a visual diagram. The flowchart illustrates how a function is invoked with specific parameters, processes the input, and returns an output. This aids in grasping the methodology behind function encapsulation and execution.
scenes.image_prompt:
flowchart TD
    A[Start Function] --> B[Receive Parameters]
    B --> C[Process Logic]
    C --> D[Return Result]
    D --> E[Function Call Ends]
